name: Precision Micropipetting
description: How to set volume, attach tips, aspirate/dispense using the two-stop technique, and maintain proper angle for accuracy.

safety.notes: Avoid touching sterile tips; maintain sterility and prevent contamination.

procedure.goal: Accurate microliter liquid handling using correct range and plunger stops.
prep: Confirm pipette range (P20/P200/P1000) and planned volume.

steps.text: Set the volume with the adjustment dial to match your protocol.
steps.warning: Do not dial past range limits; it can shift calibration and ruin accuracy.

steps.text: Attach a new disposable sterile tip by pressing the shaft into the tip in the box, then lifting out.
steps.warning: Do not touch the tip with hands or surfaces.

steps.text: To aspirate: press plunger to the FIRST STOP, dip tip into liquid, then slowly release plunger to draw liquid.
To dispense: touch tip to dispensing surface, press to the SECOND STOP, then remove tip and release plunger.
Dispose of the used tip using the tip ejector.
Maintain a vertical pipette angle for best accuracy.

common_mistakes: Not keeping the pipette vertical during aspiration

media.caption: Micropipette parts, volume ranges, two-stop technique, and proper angle.
media.alt_text: Handout page showing micropipette parts, P20/P200/P1000 volume windows, two-stop plunger technique, and vertical angle diagram.

name: Using a Centrifuge
description: Principles of centrifugation, balancing rules, and safe operating procedure for microcentrifuge tubes.

safety.notes: Do not leave until it reaches full speed and runs smoothly; only open when rotor fully stops.

procedure.goal: Safely separate sample components by density while preventing imbalance.

steps.text: Ensure tube caps are snapped shut tightly to prevent leakage during the spin.
Load tubes symmetrically (directly opposite each other) with equal mass/volume.
If spinning one tube, create a mock balance tube with equal volume of water and place it directly across.
Close internal rotor lid (if present) and main safety lid.
Set speed (RPM/RCF) and time per protocol, then press start.
Stay until full operating speed is reached and it runs smoothly without unusual noise.
Wait for complete stop before opening and unloading.

media.caption: Supernatant vs pellet and balancing patterns.
media.alt_text: Handout page illustrating centrifugation principle and example rotor balancing configurations.

name: Vortexing (Why and When)
description: How and why to vortex for homogenization, with setup and SOP (grip, contact point, angle, duration, verification).

procedure.goal: Homogenize sample so concentration is consistent across microliters.

steps.text: Set vortex mixer to Touch mode for manual control.
Set speed to Level 8–10 (high) unless your reagent is sensitive.
Hold the tube firmly near the top (cap) between thumb and forefinger.
Press bottom of tube into the center of the rubber vortex cup.
Hold the tube at a slight angle (~15°–20°) to create a stronger whirlpool effect.
Vortex 5–10 seconds for standard mixing; ~30 seconds (or until clear) for pellet resuspension.
Visually verify: liquid should reach at least halfway up the tube during vortexing.

media.caption: Vortex SOP + example vortex mixer types.
media.alt_text: Handout page describing vortex setup and steps, plus images of different vortex mixers.

name: Using a Thermocycler
description: Thermocycler checklist, common PCR stages, loading tips (dummy tubes, heated lid), and notes like ramp rate and pause guidance.

procedure.goal: Run consistent temperature cycling for PCR without evaporation or seating issues.

steps.text: Use thin-walled PCR tubes/plates (microcentrifuge tubes are too thick).
Ensure caps are clicked / film is sealed flat; evaporation is a major failure cause.
Verify reaction volume matches the software setting to ensure correct calculated sample temperatures.
If running only a few tubes, place empty dummy tubes in the four corners to balance lid pressure.
Set heated lid to 105°C to prevent condensation in caps.
Press tubes firmly into wells; air gaps insulate and disrupt ramp rate.
Use standard ramp rate (~3.0°C/sec) unless protocol says otherwise.
Avoid pausing at 95°C; if you must pause, do so during 4°C hold or 72°C extension.

media.caption: Thermocycler stages and loading notes.
media.alt_text: Handout page describing thermocycler purpose, checklist, PCR stage table, and loading/operation notes.

name: Lab Safety Basics
description: PPE expectations, labeling and hood usage, aerosol prevention, waste hierarchy, and emergency procedures.

safety.notes: Change gloves if punctured or after touching clean equipment; use hood for volatile/odorous chemicals.

procedure.goal: Reduce exposure and contamination risks; handle spills/emergencies safely.

steps.text: Wear nitrile gloves; change immediately if punctured or after touching phone/keyboard while handling samples.
Label all containers with content name, date, and initials; unlabeled containers are treated as hazardous waste.
Use fume hood for volatile/odorous chemicals; keep sash at working height for airflow.
After vortexing or thermocycling, wait ~3 seconds before opening tubes to let aerosols settle.
Dispose waste in correct containers (biohazard/sharps, glass box, general trash) per hierarchy.
Spills: minor clean with 70% ethanol or 10% bleach (depending on agent); major evacuate and notify lab manager.
Eye exposure: use eyewash/shower for at least 15 minutes.
Fire/smoke: unplug device if safe; otherwise use nearest Class BC extinguisher.

media.caption: Safety rules, waste hierarchy, and emergencies + dilution equation on same page.
media.alt_text: Handout page covering lab safety (PPE, labeling, fume hood), waste types, emergency procedures, plus dilution equation.

name: Sterility and Aseptic Technique
description: Personal prep, bench/equipment sterilization, golden rules (tip management, tube lids, aerosol control), and disposal guidance.

procedure.goal: Prevent introduction of foreign substances and avoid cross-contamination.

steps.text: Put on fresh nitrile gloves; if you touch face/phone/AR glasses, change gloves or re-sanitize.
Spray gloved hands with 70% ethanol and rub until dry.
Spray bench area with 70% ethanol and wipe with a Kimwipe before setting up racks.
Wipe pipette barrels with ethanol-dampened wipe; ensure racks are clean.
Never touch pipette tips; keep tip box closed when not actively withdrawing tips.
Minimize tube-open time: open, pipette, close immediately.
Avoid excessive blow-out force to reduce aerosols.
Eject used tips immediately into sharps/biohazard; never reuse tips across different reagents or after surface contact.

media.caption: Sterility workflow + golden rules.
media.alt_text: Handout page describing aseptic preparation, bench sterilization, sterile handling rules, and disposal notes.

name: Lab Documentation (Bench Notes)
description: What to record pre-experiment, during the protocol, and post-experiment (weights, timestamps, observations, lot numbers, deviations).

procedure.goal: Maintain precise real-time records for reproducibility and later analysis.

steps.text: Before starting: label every tube clearly and record dry weight of each empty tube to nearest 0.1 mg (0.0001 g).
During: record reagent lot numbers (if provided) and show calculations for master mix.
During: note deviations (spill, overshoot pipette volume, safety intervention) with exact step and time.
After: record final wet weight (tube + final solution) to compute actual/theoretical pipetting accuracy.
After: record start/end timestamps per sub-protocol and note visual anomalies (bubbles, cloudiness).

media.caption: Documentation checklist and required data points.
media.alt_text: Handout page listing baseline recording, real-time bench note requirements, and post-experiment data entry checklist.
